skills.migration_notes: Replace with upstream 'styx'. Feature parity. Upstream has slightly richer status documentation (refund-requested, canceled states). Minor differences.

skills.name: systems-monitor

skills.migration_notes: Arc-specific skill. Stays in instance repo during reorg. Has hardcoded refs: whoabuddy/worker-logs refs.

has_upstream_equivalent.notes: Upstream is messaging CLI (send/read/status). Local adds sensor automation, DB state tracking, welcome dedup. Orthogonal: inbox is a tool, inbox-sync is autonomous behavior.
Upstream has richer corrections subcommand and JSON output. Local is condensed. Upstream is fresher.
Local is narrative SKILL.md only (no CLI). Upstream has compose-signal, check-sources, editorial-guide commands. DELETE local, load upstream.
Upstream is generic correspondent workflow. Local adds beat ownership, rate limiting, judge-signal pre-flight, fetch-ordinals-data, brief compilation sensor. Deep coupling in cli.ts/sensor.ts.
Upstream bootstraps individual agents (wallet, registration). Local sends welcome messages to new ecosystem agents. Completely different purposes.
Upstream targets bounty.drx4.xyz (token rewards). Local targets GitHub (USD rewards). Different data sources and reward mechanisms.
Upstream is CEO operating manual (reference doc). Local adds sensor-driven queue review every 4h. Could load upstream for strategic framework.
Local is expanded version of upstream with wartime/peacetime modes. Could replace with upstream + Arc-specific addendum.
Both AES-256-GCM encrypted stores. Different schemas: local uses service/key tuples, upstream uses flat IDs. Local shares wallet master password. Migration risk high.
Upstream is on-chain ERC-8004 reputation registry. Local is off-chain BIP-322 signed reviews in SQLite. Fundamentally different architectures.
No upstream equivalent found. Local skill is standalone.
Different tools entirely. QuorumClaw is multisig orchestration via REST API with ERC-8004 reputation hooks. PSBT is lower-level PSBT signing/broadcast primitives.
Same 3 commands (get-pubkey, verify-cosig, guide). Upstream has better output examples and TXIDs. Upstream is more complete.
Upstream has full lifecycle (create, import, delete, export, rotate, switch, multi-wallet). Local is single-wallet Arc wrapper. Upstream is the complete reference.
Local is deprecated duplicate of defi-bitflow. Delete bucket confirmed.
Upstream has full DEX ops (HODLMM, Keeper automation, liquidity). Local adds sensor-driven signal filing + spread detection. Complementary: local sensor logic could enhance upstream.
Local adds budget enforcement (50 STX cap), SQLite position tracking, P&L reporting, 6h sensor. Upstream is bare API wrapper. LOCAL COULD CONTRIBUTE BACK budget enforcement layer.
No direct upstream equivalent. Upstream 'defi' skill covers Zest but as part of broader DeFi skill. Local is dedicated Zest lending wrapper.
Upstream unifies identity+reputation+validation. Local is modular (identity only). Trade-off: modularity vs discoverability.
Identical command set. Upstream has better JSON output examples and pagination docs.
Trust aggregation is a read-only subset of reputation. Could merge into erc8004-reputation.
Same command set. Upstream has better output examples and progressive update docs.
Upstream manages ERC-8004 identity NFTs on-chain. Local validates local filesystem identity consistency (SOUL.md/MEMORY.md drift detection). Completely different layers.
Upstream has full lifecycle: deposit, cancel, close, settle, user-activity, cycles-history. Local missing ~50% of functionality. Upstream is significantly more complete.
Local has sensor (fee spikes, incoming BTC). Upstream has CLI (tx-status, address-history, mempool-stats). Complementary: could merge into one skill with both.
Upstream 'maximumsats-wot' has cli.ts+sensor.ts but no SKILL.md. Local is fully documented with trust-score, sybil-check, neighbors, config. LOCAL IS AHEAD.
Different tools. Local does market data aggregation and signal filing. Upstream does inscription creation (commit/reveal). Not comparable.
Upstream has full CLI (list-pots, join-pot, start-pot, claim-rewards, cancel). Local adds sensor-driven auto-join. Could use upstream CLI + local sensor.
Feature parity. Upstream has slightly richer status documentation (refund-requested, canceled states). Minor differences.
